structured_payload_candidate.summary: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall modulate to maintain building pressure at 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) with output 0-100%. Loop enabled when supply and return fans for any unit within the pressure zone are proven ON and the minimum outdoor air damper is open. Exhaust dampers closed with loop output zero otherwise. All exhaust damper and return fan static pressure setpoints for units in an associated pressure zone shall be sequenced based on building pressure control loop output signal as shown in Figure 5.16.10.5: from 0% to 50%, modulate exhaust dampers from 0% to 100% open; from 51% to 100%, reset return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint from RFDSPmin at 50% loop output to RFDSPmax at 100% loop output.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply and return fans proven ON and min OA damper open.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: P-only loop maintains 12 Pa; sequence exhaust dampers 0-100% for 0-50% output; reset RFDSP setpoint from RFDSPmin to RFDSPmax for 51-100% output.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.16.10.5. A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall modulate to maintain the building pressure at a setpoint of 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) with an output ranging from 0% to 100%. The loop shall be enabled when the supply and return fans for any unit within the pressure zone are proven ON and the minimum outdoor air damper is open. The exhaust dampers shall be closed with loop output set to zero otherwise. All exhaust damper and return fan static pressure setpoints for units in an associated pressure zone shall be sequenced based on building pressure control loop output signal, as shown in Figure 5.16.10.5.
a. From 0% to 50%, the building pressure control loop shall modulate the exhaust dampers from 0% to 100% open.
b. From 51% to 100%, the building pressure control loop shall reset the return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint from RFDSPmin at 50% loop output to RFDSPmax at 100% of loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Differential Airflow Setpoint S-R-DIFF*
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The active differential airflow setpoint S-R-DIFF* shall be S-R-DIFF adjusted by the sum of the area component of the breathing zone outdoor air flow rate of zones in Occupied Mode relative to that in all zones served by the system. For ASHRAE 62.1: S-R-DIFF* = S-R-DIFF * (∑Vbz-A occupied zones / ∑Vbz-A all zones). For Title 24: S-R-DIFF* = S-R-DIFF * (∑Varea-min occupied zones / ∑Varea-min all zones).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 1
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If supply air temperature drops below 4.4°C (40°F) for 5 minutes, send two heating hot-water plant requests, override outdoor air damper to minimum position, and modulate heating coil to maintain SAT at least 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT rises above 7°C (45°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send HW plant requests; override OA damper to min; modulate heating coil to maintain SAT ≥ 6°C (42°F). Disable when SAT > 7°C (45°F) for 5 min.

structured_payload_candidate.summary: If supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 3
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Supply fan, return/relief fan, outdoor air damper, cooling coil valve, CHW pump, heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon signal from freeze-stat or if SAT drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fans, close outdoor air damper, open cooling-coil valve to 100%, energize CHW pump system, send two heating hot-water plant requests, modulate heating coil to maintain the higher of SAT or MAT at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating unit shut down by freeze protection. If triggered by low air temperature sensor, remain in effect until reset by software switch from operator's workstation.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Freeze-stat signal OR SAT < 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 min OR SAT < 1°C (34°F) for 5 min.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Shut down fans; close OA damper; open cooling coil valve 100%; energize CHW pump; send HW plant requests; modulate heating coil to maintain higher of SAT or MAT at 27°C (80°F); set Level 2 alarm. If sensor triggered, require software reset.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.16.12.3. Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, open the cooling-coil valve to 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Also send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection.
a. If a freeze-protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator's workstation. (If a freeze-stat with a physical reset switch is used instead, there shall be no software reset switch.)
